_wizard.includedScope: Full interior design, custom joinery, lighting design, art display lighting, soft furnishings, terrace landscape design, smart home integration.
_wizard.excludedScope: Building structural changes, exterior facade, common area work.
_wizard.designStyle: Quiet Luxury with Indian Modern accents
_wizard.keyAspirations: A penthouse that frames Sara's contemporary Indian art collection while feeling warm and lived-in. Indoor-outdoor flow. No marble overload — focus on warm woods, fabric walls, and subtle metalwork.

externalStakeholders.projectNotes: Atlas team led by Mahesh Krishnan. On site since Mar 2026.
Klove + Magari curated. Living room chandelier on 14-week lead.
Sahil Bharti workshop in Khar. Walnut + brushed brass theme.

specs.label: Body

projectApprovals.title: DD Drawings — All Levels (Approved)
projectApprovals.decisionNotes: All 38 drawings approved. Two minor markups: master closet door swing + powder room WC position.

projectApprovals.title: Quiet Luxury direction approved. Sara requested 30% more brass detailing. CMF locked: warm woods (walnut + oak), travertine + statuario, fabric walls, brushed brass. No marble overload. Materials to highlight art collection.
projectApprovals.clientNotes: Decided in: Concept Approval Meeting on 21 Jan 2026.

comms.subject: Concept Moodboard ready for review
comms.preview: Hi Sara, the Quiet Luxury moodboard is ready. Three sub-directions explored — wanted to walk you through them in person. Free this Friday afternoon?

comms.subject: Loved the brass details
comms.preview: Meher hi! Both Arjun and I really loved the brass touches in the moodboard — can we add more of it across the design? Especially for the bar nook and library wall.

comms.subject: Question about master bath marble
comms.preview: Quick one — for the Calacatta Viola in master bath, can we see physical samples this weekend? I want to make sure the purple veining is consistent across the slabs you've identified.

comms.subject: Site progress update — Week 11
comms.preview: Hi Arjun, attaching site photos from this week. MEP first-fix is now complete across both levels. Walnut veneer panel installation begins next Tuesday. Two minor items needing your wife's decision attached.

comms.subject: Atlas crew query on terrace waterproofing
comms.preview: Riya didi, Atlas team asking if waterproofing test results can be shared with their site engineer before IPE order is placed. They want documentation for warranty.

siteUpdates.title: Demolition + structural strip-out complete
siteUpdates.description: All 3 levels stripped to bare structure. Existing partition walls demolished as per layout. No structural concerns. Ready for first-fix MEP.
siteUpdates.author: Devansh Iyer

siteUpdates.title: MEP first-fix complete — both indoor levels
siteUpdates.description: Electrical, plumbing, HVAC ducting, AV cabling all routed. Pressure tests passed. Photos shared with client. Walnut veneer team scheduled to begin Tuesday May 12.
siteUpdates.author: Devansh Iyer

decisions.text: Project scope confirmed: 3-level penthouse, 7800 sqft. Quiet Luxury aesthetic with Indian Modern accents. Sara is the final aesthetic call. Project completion target: 15 Nov 2026. Total fee ₹68L.

Quiet Luxury direction approved. Sara requested 30% more brass detailing. CMF locked: warm woods (walnut + oak), travertine + statuario, fabric walls, brushed brass. No marble overload. Materials to highlight art collection.

actionItems.text: More brass detailing - CMF locked: warm woods (walnut + oak), travertine + statuario, fabric walls, brushed brass. No marble overload. Materials to highlight art collection.

decisions.text: All 38 DD drawings approved. Two markups: master closet door swing changed to right-hand, powder room WC moved 200mm to gain ventilation clearance. Lighting selections confirmed including Magari chandelier on 16-week lead.

BOQ totalling ₹2.85Cr (excluding design fee) approved. Atlas awarded civil + finishing. Sahil Bharti awarded custom joinery. Klove + Magari sourcing approved. Procurement schedule to track 18-week lead items: chandelier, kitchen, wardrobes.

moms.title: Site Walkthrough — MEP First Fix Sign-off

decisions.text: MEP first-fix signed off across Lower + Upper levels. No reworks needed. Walnut veneer installation cleared to begin May 12. Terrace waterproofing test scheduled for May 22 before IPE decking begins.

_wizard.includedScope: Full interior design, custom joinery, lighting, soft furnishings, art curation.

externalStakeholders.projectNotes: Awarded after 3-vendor RFQ. Atlas PM is Mahesh Krishnan.

siteUpdates.title: Ground floor demo started — load-bearing wall identified
siteUpdates.author: Devansh Iyer (Site Manager)
siteUpdates.clientNotes: Demo work started on schedule. The internal wall between living and dining is being assessed for removal — structural consultant visit booked for Friday.
siteUpdates.internalNotes: Wall is load-bearing, contractor flagged column thickening required before removal. ₹40-50K extra in steel reinforcement, will surface in next variation order. Atlas Build PM aware.

siteUpdates.title: Italian marble slab inspection — Statuario
siteUpdates.author: Meher Kapoor (Designer)
siteUpdates.clientNotes: Inspected 4 slabs at the vendor warehouse. Selected 2 with the cleanest veining for the living room floor — book-matching planned at the central lounge zone. Photos attached for your records.
siteUpdates.internalNotes: Vendor pricing held at ₹950/sqft despite supply tightness. Backup quarry contact saved if these don't hold cure. Slab thickness verified at 18mm — within spec.

siteUpdates.title: Master bedroom wardrobe — joinery delay flag
siteUpdates.author: Devansh Iyer (Site Manager)
siteUpdates.clientNotes: Master bedroom wardrobe joinery is delayed by approximately one week. Vendor is sourcing replacement Hettich hinges that meet the original soft-close spec. New delivery date: 18 March. We will recover the lost time in the parallel-track painting work.
siteUpdates.internalNotes: Vendor screwed up the original Hettich PO — wrong hinge series shipped from Pune. They're absorbing the cost of replacement (₹14K worth of hardware) plus expediting fee. If this happens again with their 2nd commitment we should escalate to their MD.

comms.author: Riya Sharma (Project Manager)
comms.body: Kicking off internal coord — Atlas needs the structural consultant report by EOW or wall demo gets delayed. I'll chase Anil tomorrow.

comms.author: Riya Sharma (Project Manager)
comms.body: Hi Aakash — checking in ahead of Friday's site visit. Confirming 11am, our structural consultant Anil joining. Brief agenda: walk the ground floor demo zone, review the proposed wall removal, sign off on column thickening if everyone's aligned. We'll have the marble samples on site as well for your selection.
comms.internalAnnotation: Worth flagging to client team that wall removal is now contingent on consultant signoff — manage expectations.

comms.author: Atlas Build (Vendor)
comms.body: Confirmed Friday 11am attendance. We'll have demolition crew on standby — if signoff happens that day we can start by Monday. Steel reinforcement quote attached separately, will share by Wednesday EOD.

comms.author: Meher Kapoor (Designer)
comms.body: Need a heads up — vendor is dragging on the wardrobe Hettich hinge replacement. I'm holding the door delivery until they confirm new ETA. Riya, fyi for client comms.

comms.author: Riya Sharma (Project Manager)
comms.body: Aakash — small update on the master bedroom wardrobe. Vendor had a hardware issue and we're holding delivery by ~1 week to make sure the soft-close mechanism is exactly to spec. Posting a site update with details. No project-level timeline impact, we're absorbing it via parallel work.
comms.internalAnnotation: Dont mention vendor name in client comms unless asked.

moms.title: Concept review + material direction signoff

agendaItems.text: Walk-through of concept boards (Heritage / Coastal / Modern)
Layout discussion — living room expansion option

decisions.text: Heritage concept selected as primary direction. Coastal elements to be incorporated in master bedroom only.
Italian marble (Statuario) approved for living room flooring. Vendor sourcing to begin.
Living room wall removal — exploration approved, contingent on structural consultant signoff.

actionItems.text: Engage structural consultant for load-bearing wall assessment
Source 4-6 marble slab samples from vendor for in-person review
Share revised DD program with milestones

moms.clientNotes: Excellent session — alignment on direction and material palette. Heritage with Sabyasachi/Nilaya wallpaper as the foyer accent. Marble selection to follow vendor visit. Wall-removal feasibility next on the critical path.
moms.internalNotes: Vikram (father) absent — Aakash mentioned he'll need a separate walkthrough. Priya pushed back gently on the marble selection rate (₹950/sqft); managed it by emphasizing the book-matching technique value. Meher to prep alternate options at ₹650-750 range as backup.

moms.title: Site visit + structural consultant walkthrough

agendaItems.text: Marble slab review (3 options on site)

decisions.text: Wall removal approved with column thickening. Steel reinforcement spec to be issued by Atlas next week.
Statuario slabs — Slab 1 and Slab 2 selected for living room book-match.
Demolition cleared to start Monday Feb 17.

actionItems.text: Issue steel reinforcement quote with timeline and cost
Reserve Slab 1 + Slab 2 with vendor; advance ₹50K
Prepare variation order documentation for column thickening cost

moms.clientNotes: Productive site visit. Wall removal cleared with structural mitigation. Marble slabs locked. Demolition starts Monday — site manager Devansh will share progress updates throughout. Variation order for steel reinforcement coming separately.
moms.internalNotes: Atlas tried to inflate the steel quote on the spot — verbal ₹85K, written quote needed. If they come back high, Anil has a recommended fabricator we can use as comp. Aakash didnt push back on the variation cost — keep that line open without overplaying.

externalStakeholders.projectNotes: La Marzocco Linea PB selected. Lead time 12 weeks ex-Italy.
Engaged for sound dampening on the high-ceiling seating area.

siteUpdates.title: Counter mockup approved by Kavi team
siteUpdates.author: Meher Kapoor (Designer)
siteUpdates.clientNotes: The counter mockup was approved by your team in today's walkthrough. We're proceeding with full production. The brass inlay detail looks even better in person — final installation expected by end of March.
siteUpdates.internalNotes: Client loved it. Vendor needs to commit to the brass supplier (Pune lead-time concern). Worth pre-ordering the inlay strip if final go.

siteUpdates.title: Mezzanine staircase — design revision required
siteUpdates.author: Devansh Iyer (Site Manager)
siteUpdates.clientNotes: During on-site review we found the original staircase design conflicts with the new BMC code on rise/tread proportions. Working on a revised design now — will have updated drawings to share by Friday. No timeline impact.
siteUpdates.internalNotes: Architect missed the BMC update from Jan 2026. Embarrassing. Revising drawings free of charge. Risk: client may ask why this wasn't caught — have the response ready.

comms.author: Devansh Iyer (Site Manager)
comms.body: Counter mockup approved on Saturday. Brass inlay supplier (Pune) is the long-pole — need to commit by Wednesday or we slip cabinetry install.

comms.author: Meher Kapoor (Designer)
comms.body: Hi Kavi team — confirming the counter mockup signoff yesterday, thanks for making the time. We're proceeding with full production. Brass inlay strip ordered. Targeting end of March for the install. I'll share next milestone update by Friday.

moms.title: Counter mockup walkthrough + production go-ahead

decisions.text: Counter mockup approved as-is. Full production cleared.
Brass inlay strip — Pune supplier confirmed. Pre-order placed.

actionItems.text: Place full PO for counter joinery production
Confirm install date in cabinetry schedule

moms.clientNotes: Counter mockup approved. Brass inlay locked. Production starts immediately, install targeted end of March.
moms.internalNotes: Vendor was nervous about the brass inlay timeline. Pre-ordering inlay was a good move — saved us a 2-week slip.

MasterActivities.description: Initial client meeting, scope confirmation, contract signing.
Physical measurement, photographs, site condition documentation.
Document client preferences, lifestyle, must-haves and constraints.
Visual direction — colors, materials, references, feeling.
2-3 alternative floor plans for client review.
Initial material library — finishes, fabrics, stone, wood samples.
Quick volumetric renders for spatial feel — not photorealistic.
Order-of-magnitude budget estimate to validate concept feasibility.
Final floor plans with all dimensions, fixtures, equipment placement.
Comprehensive specification — every finish, every supplier, every code.
Photorealistic visualisations of key spaces for client signoff.
Custom millwork, built-ins, FF&E specifications.
Reflected ceiling plan, fixture selection, switching/control logic.
Plans, elevations, sections at 1:50 / 1:25.
Detailed shop drawings for every custom joinery piece.
Electrical, plumbing, HVAC coordination drawings.
Item-by-item quantification from drawings.
Get quotes from approved vendors for each line item.
Apply markup, finalise client-facing BOQ document.
Final vendor shortlisting and PO award.
Issue purchase orders and track lead times.
Physical sample sign-off before bulk order.
Demolition, masonry, plastering, structural modifications.
Custom millwork installation and finishing.
Wall finishes, flooring, painting.
Electrical, plumbing, HVAC installation and commissioning.
Furniture, fixtures, equipment placement; final styling.
Joint walkthrough with client to log defects.
Fix flagged items and re-inspection.
Warranty documents, maintenance manuals, vendor contacts.
Professional photography for studio portfolio and client records.

Coordinated MEP drawings for execution.
Internal quantity calculations from drawings.
Side-by-side comparison of vendor quotes per line item.
Final client-facing BOQ with markup, terms, schedule.
Photographed list of every defect to be resolved.
Care instructions, warranty docs, vendor contacts.
Professional photography — for studio portfolio and client.

MasterProjectTemplates.description: Full turnkey for 4-5 BHK villas. 12-month average build cycle.
MasterProjectTemplates.includedScopeDefault: Full interior design, custom joinery, lighting design, soft furnishings, art curation, end-to-end procurement and execution.
MasterProjectTemplates.excludedScopeDefault: Civil structural changes, exterior landscaping, smart home integration, swimming pool design.

MasterProjectTemplates.description: 5-month standard café fitout from bare shell. Includes equipment specification.
MasterProjectTemplates.includedScopeDefault: Layout design, joinery, lighting, signage, FF&E procurement, equipment specification, on-site supervision.
MasterProjectTemplates.excludedScopeDefault: Civil works (handled by landlord), brand identity design, menu engineering.

MasterProjectTemplates.linkedServiceType: Design + Technical Drawing + Consultation + Project Management
MasterProjectTemplates.description: For 30,000+ sqft corporate spaces. Multi-floor phased delivery with PMC.
MasterProjectTemplates.includedScopeDefault: Workplace strategy, space planning, design development, working drawings, BoQ, vendor management, project management with weekly reporting.
MasterProjectTemplates.excludedScopeDefault: IT cabling design, AV integration, base-build modifications, security system integration.

MasterProjectTemplates.description: High-end penthouses. Design + technical only — client engages separate contractor.
MasterProjectTemplates.includedScopeDefault: Concept design, design development, full working drawings, material specification, vendor recommendations, periodic site reviews (max 6 visits).
MasterProjectTemplates.excludedScopeDefault: Procurement, on-site supervision, contractor coordination, BoQ negotiations.

MasterProjectTemplates.linkedServiceType: Design + Technical Drawing + Consultation + Project Management
MasterProjectTemplates.description: Phased delivery across multiple floors / wings / buildings. Sequential handovers, longer timelines.
MasterProjectTemplates.includedScopeDefault: Phase-wise design, working drawings per phase, BoQ per phase, vendor coordination across phases, sequential handover protocol, monthly reporting per phase.
MasterProjectTemplates.excludedScopeDefault: Furniture procurement (FF&E), branded kitchen equipment, AV/smart home, exterior architecture changes.

Multi-floor corporate: reception, workspace, meeting rooms, cabins, cafeteria, services.
Duplex penthouse with terrace deck. Public + private + wet + outdoor.
Compact urban flat with public, private, wet zones.

MasterClients.notes: Original brief was contemporary; family pushed for warmer materials at concept.
Owns Kavi Coffee chain — 3 outlets currently, expanding to 6 by 2027. Fast decision-maker.
Family-run jewellery business. Wife is interior taste-maker.
Decisions go through Workplace Strategy + Facilities. Single sign-off rare.
Director signs off on design; finance team handles payments separately.
Family business — manages Singhania Group. Tower fitout for new HQ.
Returning client. Earlier did Khanna Vacation Home in Goa with us in 2023. Arjun runs a private equity firm; Sara is an art collector. Strong eye for materials, decisive.

MasterVendors.notes: Reliable for residential turnkey. Civil + finishing in-house, joinery via subcontract.
Lighting consultancy — selection + reflected ceiling. Premium work, slow timelines.
Specialty supplier for cafés — espresso machines, grinders, bar gear.
PMC for large corporate fitouts. Manages on behalf of corporate clients.
Tier-1 contractor — typically used by developer clients (Hiranandani, Lodha).
Acoustic consultancy — typically engaged for cafés, offices, music rooms.

rows.notes: Specialised in joinery and veneer work.

rows.notes: Premium price tier; long lead time from Italy.

MasterQuestionnaireTemplates.name: Home Owners — Standard
MasterQuestionnaireTemplates.introText: Thanks for considering us for your home. A few quick questions so we can prepare a thoughtful first conversation. 10 minutes, promise.
MasterQuestionnaireTemplates.outroText: Thank you! We have everything we need to prepare. One of our designers will reach out within 24 hours to set up a call.

MasterQuestionnaireTemplates.name: Developers — Standard
MasterQuestionnaireTemplates.introText: Thanks for getting in touch. We work with developers on show flats, amenity blocks, common areas, and full project design. A few questions so the first meeting is sharp.
MasterQuestionnaireTemplates.outroText: Thank you. Our principal designer will review and respond within 48 hours with a proposed scope and timeline.

MasterQuestionnaireTemplates.name: Office SMBs — Standard
MasterQuestionnaireTemplates.introText: Great that you are thinking about your space. Offices for small teams need a different approach from corporates — let us understand yours.
MasterQuestionnaireTemplates.outroText: Thank you. We will share two or three reference projects in your size range, then schedule a 30-minute consult.

MasterQuestionnaireTemplates.name: Corporate HQ / HO — Standard
MasterQuestionnaireTemplates.introText: Thank you for shortlisting us. Corporate HQs have layered stakeholders — these questions help us prepare for that complexity from day one.
MasterQuestionnaireTemplates.outroText: Thank you. Our principal team will prepare a tailored response within 5 working days.

MasterQuestionnaireTemplates.name: Retail & Hospitality — Standard
MasterQuestionnaireTemplates.introText: Retail and hospitality spaces are part theatre, part workplace. These questions help us understand both.
MasterQuestionnaireTemplates.outroText: Thank you. We will share a relevant mood board and reference work within 48 hours.

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: Just to confirm — this enquiry is for your home? If so, what kind?
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.whyItMatters: Validates the studio assumption about property type before everything else.

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: What stage is the home at right now?
options: Under construction, design needed before move-in
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.whyItMatters: Defines whether this is interior design, renovation, or design-during-construction. Major scope-shaper.

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: Roughly how large is the home? (built-up area in sqft)
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.whyItMatters: Auto-derives Size tier (Small/Medium/Large/XL) for siteSize master.

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: How many bedrooms?
Who lives here, and who is deciding?
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.whyItMatters: Reveals decision-maker dynamics and lifestyle needs.

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: What is your design instinct? Pick the closest 2-3.
options: Don't know yet — that's what the designer is for
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.whyItMatters: Style match — early indicator of compatibility.

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: Have you worked with an architect or interior designer before?
options: Doing parts ourselves, want a designer for the rest
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.whyItMatters: First-timers need more hand-holding; "ended badly" needs sensitivity.

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: What is your realistic budget range for design + execution? (We will not share this — it just helps us not waste your time.)
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.whyItMatters: THE single biggest qualifier. Studios that do not take small-budget projects need to filter out early.

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: When do you want to start the design work?
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.whyItMatters: Urgency. "Just exploring" is a low-priority lead.

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: When do you want to move in / complete?
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.whyItMatters: Realism check — match start vs end.

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: Which areas of the home matter most to you? (Pick top 3)
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.whyItMatters: Helps scope what to prioritise in first sketches.

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: Any non-negotiables? (e.g., must keep this sofa, vastu, pet-friendly, allergy-sensitive materials, accessibility)
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.placeholder: Tell us anything that must stay or must change...

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: How did you hear about us?
Your name, phone, and email so we can reach back?
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.whyItMatters: Contact only — not scored, but required.

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: City + neighbourhood (so we know if travel is involved)
What type of development is this?
What is the scope you need us for?
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.whyItMatters: Defines the engagement type — sample flat is very different from full common areas.

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: Total project size?
Stage of construction?
Who is the typical end-customer for this project?
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.whyItMatters: Design aesthetic must match buyer tier.

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: What is the design budget for what we would handle?
When do you need design ready by?
Have you worked with interior design studios before?
options: Have an in-house team, need supplementary

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: Who is the primary decision-maker we would be presenting to?
What part of the design intent is most critical?
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.whyItMatters: Reveals what the developer cares about.

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: Are there specific certifications required?
Reference projects you admire (link, name, or describe)
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.placeholder: Project names, Instagram handles, or descriptions...

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: Procurement model?
options: We hand off design, you execute

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: Company name + your role + contact
Project location + city
What kind of space is this?
What does your business do?
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.placeholder: e.g. SaaS startup, accounting practice, architecture studio...

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: How many people will work here at peak?
Total space size (sqft)
Is the space ready, or under construction?
Owned or leased? If leased, how many years left?
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.whyItMatters: Investment justification — short leases = lighter design.

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: What zones do you need? (Pick all that apply)
What is the design + build budget?
When do you need to move in / start operations?
Design instinct / vibe?
Brand colours / logo — should design reflect them?
Most important outcome from this space? (Pick top 2)
options: Reduce real estate cost per head

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: Who decides?
First or repeat office?
Company name, your name, role, phone, email
City + neighbourhood of the space
Is this a new HQ, a refresh, or a regional office?
Total area (sqft)
Number of floors?
Total headcount the space serves at peak?
Stage of the space?
What is the design fees + execution budget?
Procurement model?
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.whyItMatters: Engagement type — tender is heavy commitment.

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: Who are the stakeholders we would present to?
What zones / typologies are needed?
Certifications required?
When does the space need to be operational?
Phased rollout or single delivery?
Design intent?
Reference projects (yours or competitors') you would want to benchmark
Specific compliance / security requirements?
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.whyItMatters: E.g., banks have data-room norms, pharma has access control.
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.placeholder: e.g. ISO 27001, BFSI norms, pharma GMP...

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: Company name, your name, role, RFP ID if applicable, phone, email
Address of the space
What is the format?
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.whyItMatters: Sub-type — kitchen-heavy vs display-heavy needs differ massively.

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: Is this a first location, or part of a chain?
Total area (sqft)
Seating capacity OR daily footfall target
What is the space currently?
What is the design + build budget?
Target opening date?
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.whyItMatters: Retail/hospitality has hard launch deadlines — major qualifier.

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: Brand direction — already set or still defining?
options: Just an idea, need help building brand

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: Style direction?
What experience should a customer remember?
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.whyItMatters: Reveals storytelling capacity of the client.
MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.placeholder: In your words — what should they walk away feeling?

MasterQuestionnaireQuestions.text: What zones are critical?
Specific operational constraints?
Who decides design?
Existing locations / brand reference (link or photos)
Lease length?
Brand name, your name, role, phone, email, Instagram handle
Address of the space + nearest landmark
